chunks.text: Ptolemy II Installation Ptolemy II Installation See the Ptolemy II 2.0 release notes for details about this release. Ptiny Ptolemy II is available in two versions: Ptiny - which can be used to build models in CT, DE, FSM, PN and SDF using built-i

odels in CT, DE, FSM, PN and SDF using built-in actors. Full - which includes other domains and support for developing custom actors. The Ptiny version is primarily for the Windows platform, it does not include a start up script for Vergil fo

t does not include a start up script for Vergil for non-Windows platforms. Installation instructions for the Ptiny version can be found online at http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/ptolemyII . The Full version is for Windows and Unix platforms. The i

version is for Windows and Unix platforms. The instructions on this page concern installing the Full version. Instructions for the impatient Install JDK1.3.1, add the jdk bin directory to your path so that javac and java are available, and have the

so that javac and java are available, and have the make command available. Set the PTII environment variable to the top level Ptolemy directory, that is the directory above this directory bash PTII=c:\\Program\ Files\\Ptolemy\\Ptolemy\ II\ 2.0 expor

\\Program\ Files\\Ptolemy\\Ptolemy\ II\ 2.0 export PTII csh setenv PTII /users/yourname/ptII Run configure cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure Ptolemy is shipped with .class and .jar files, but the $PTII/bin/scripts needs to be updated. The safes

$PTII/bin/scripts needs to be updated. The safest thing to do is to run make install on the entire tree: make fast install The fast rule runs javac .. *.java in the various directories and the install rule updates scripts, builds jar files and run

all rule updates scripts, builds jar files and runs javadoc. Start vergil $PTII/bin/vergil Requirements The PTII variable The PTII environment variable is used to refer to the directory that contains the Ptolemy II tree. Setting PTII is covered in th

the Ptolemy II tree. Setting PTII is covered in the Unix and Windows sections below. Java Ptolemy II has been developed under JDK1.3.1, which is available from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.3/ All versions of Ptolemy II 2.0 require Java 1.3 or later. Ja

ns of Ptolemy II 2.0 require Java 1.3 or later. Java 1.3 is preferred, the code generator will not work with JDK 1.4 The Java download is available in several formats. JRE - Java Runtime Environment Runtime only If you are planning on only using the

untime only If you are planning on only using the prebuilt Ptolemy II actors, you can download the JRE. JDK - Java Development Kit Runtime and development tools like the Java compiler javac If you are planning on writing your own actors or recompilin

planning on writing your own actors or recompiling from sources, download the JDK. If you install the JDK, be sure that the bin directory is in your path so that the javac and java commands are available. Java Plug-in The applet tags have been conf

lable. Java Plug-in The applet tags have been configured to require JDK1.3, so to run the applets in Netscape 4.7 or Internet Explorer 5.0 under Windows, you will need to use the Java Plug-in from http://java.sun.com/products/plugin/ Currently, the J

p://java.sun.com/products/plugin/ Currently, the Java Plug-in 1.3 is included in the JDK 1.3 download for Windows. If you are running under Solaris 2.6 or 2.7, you can download a separate plug-in or a complete Netscape installation. If your platform

complete Netscape installation. If your platform is not supported by the plug-in, you can run the applets with the JDK appletviewer command. See the Plug-in section of the Ptolemy II Troubleshooting guide for details. Optional packages that are ship

guide for details. Optional packages that are shipped with Ptolemy Jacl Jacl is a 100% Java implementation of Tcl that we use for testing Ptolemy II. Each Java directory contains a test subdirectory that contains Tcl files that use Jacl to test the

that contains Tcl files that use Jacl to test the Ptolemy II Java code. Jacl is only necessary if you are planning on running the Ptolemy II test suite. We ship a customized version of Jacl called Ptjacl. The primary difference between Ptjacl and Jac

acl. The primary difference between Ptjacl and Jacl1.1 is that Ptjacl is shipped as one jar file. The Ptjacl jar file at $PTII/lib/ptjacl.jar is shipped with Ptolemy II. For more information about Jacl and Tcl Blend, see Activestate.com Jacl and Tcl

l and Tcl Blend, see Activestate.com Jacl and Tcl Blend page . Christopher Hylands' Java Page. java:: man page Diva Diva is a visualization package used by some of the demos. The Ptolemy II distribution include $PTII/lib/diva.jar . The Diva sources a

on include $PTII/lib/diva.jar . The Diva sources and documentation are available at http://www.gigascale.org/diva . Optional packages not shipped with Ptolemy make Ptolemy II has been built with GNU make 3.77, but does not use GNU make extensions, so

ake 3.77, but does not use GNU make extensions, so GNU make is not required. Ptolemy II has also been built with Solaris 2.8 /usr/ccs/bin/make . It seems that the make that is included with SGI Irix 6.5 may have problems, the workaround is to instal

6.5 may have problems, the workaround is to installed GNU make. Java3d The 3-D graphics (GR) domain demos require Java3d, which can be installed from http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/3D/ . You need not install Java3d if you are not planning o

need not install Java3d if you are not planning on using GR domain. If you plan on recompiling the GR domain, then install the Java 3D SDK and runtime. If you plan on only running the GR domain demos, then installing only the Java 3D runtime should

s, then installing only the Java 3D runtime should be sufficient. We have verified the demos with the Java 3D 1.2.1_04 SDK (DirectX Version). Note that JDK1.4.0 requires Java 3D 1.2.1_04. If you run with Java 3D 1.2.1_03 and JDK1.4.0, the GR demos w

with Java 3D 1.2.1_03 and JDK1.4.0, the GR demos will fail to initialize and you will see the message: Fail to create back buffer - DDERR_INALIDPARAM The Java 3D 1.2.1_04 release notes say that this bug: 4470749 Java3D fail to initialize underlying O

ug: 4470749 Java3D fail to initialize underlying OGL/D3D library in jdk1.4 was fixed in Java 3D 1.2.1_04 Matlab Ptolemy II includes an interface to Matlab that was developed by Zoltan Kemenczy and Sean Simmons of Research in Motion Limited . The Ma

ean Simmons of Research in Motion Limited . The Matlab interface works only under Windows and it requires that Matlab be installed on the local machine. The Matlab interface should work with version of Matlab from R11 (5.3) and up to and including R

of Matlab from R11 (5.3) and up to and including R12.1 (6.1) The Matlab Expression actor can access Matlab methods to process matrices and plot data using the Matlab plotter. A demo can be found at $PTII/ptolemy/matlab/demo/MatlabExpression/MatlabExp

TII/ptolemy/matlab/demo/MatlabExpression/MatlabExpression.xml Compiling the Matlab interface The Matlab interface will only work under Windows. If you do not have gcc or dlltool installed, you can install them as part of the Cygwin installation The M

tall them as part of the Cygwin installation The Matlab interface is implemented using the Java Native Interface (JNI). When $PTII/configure is run, it look in the user path for a binary called matlab , determines the location of the Matlab extern/i

b , determines the location of the Matlab extern/include/libeng.dif file and then checks for gcc and dlltool . If these components were not found at when configure was run, then you can rerun configure, remake and run the demo with: cd $PTII rm confi

e, remake and run the demo with: cd $PTII rm config.*; ./configure cd $PTII/ptolemy/matlab make $PTII/bin/vergil ./demo/MatlabExpression/MatlabExpression JavaCC JavaCC is the Java Compiler Compiler, which similar to yacc and lex . JavaCC is only nece

hich similar to yacc and lex . JavaCC is only necessary if you modify the expression parser in ptolemy/data/expr . As of 1/00, Ptolemy II was using JavaCC 1.1 To install JavaCC, follow the instructions below Download JavaCC1_1.class from http://www.s

s below Download JavaCC1_1.class from http://www.sun.com/forte/ffj/resources/contributedcontent.html Set your CLASSPATH to . setenv CLASSPATH . cd to the directory where the JavaCC .class file was downloaded to. Run java on the file. Note that you s

nloaded to. Run java on the file. Note that you should not include the .class suffix in the command line. java Java1_1 $PTII/configure looks for JavaCC in the $PTII/vendors/sun/JavaCC directory. If configure does not find JavaCC there, then it look

configure does not find JavaCC there, then it looks for the javacc binary your path. You may also use the --with-javacc=DIR configure argument. The best place to install JavaCC is $PTII/vendors/sun/javaCC Rebuilding The Ptolemy II distribution inclu

avaCC Rebuilding The Ptolemy II distribution includes the compiled Java .class files and the javadoc output, so it is not necessary to 'build' or run make to use Ptolemy II. Unix If you would like to rebuild from the Java sources under Unix, you shou

rebuild from the Java sources under Unix, you should set the PTII environment variable in your environment. Under C Shell: setenv PTII /users/ptII You may want to add that line to your ~/.cshrc file. To configure the makefiles, run $PTII/configure ,

To configure the makefiles, run $PTII/configure , which is a GNU autoconf script that looks for your Java compiler, reads in $PTII/mk/ptII.mk.in and generates $PTII/mk/ptII.mk : cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure $PTII/configure creates $PTII/con

ig.* ./configure $PTII/configure creates $PTII/config.status and $PTII/config.cache . If you would like to reconfigure Ptolemy II to use a different Java compiler, set your path so that the appropriate compiler is first in your path, then remove con

te compiler is first in your path, then remove config.status and rerun configure. You may also use the --with-java= configure command line argument. For more information, run configure -help . To build: make fast install Windows Ptolemy II was deve

ild: make fast install Windows Ptolemy II was developed under Windows 2000. We have successfully installed Ptolemy II under Windows '98, but if you have problems under Windows '98 we recommend upgrading Windows 2000. To build Ptolemy II under Windo

ding Windows 2000. To build Ptolemy II under Windows 2000: Install JDK1.3.1 from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.3/ Note that the code generation facility will not work with JDK1 Be sure to add the bin directory to your path so that javac and java are ava

ectory to your path so that javac and java are available. Install the Cygwin toolkit from http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/ Complete installation instructions can be found at http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/faq , The Cygwin installer tends to change

/cygwin/faq , The Cygwin installer tends to change over time, but below is a summary of the steps necessary: Create an empty directory Download http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/setup.exe and save it in the empty directory Click on setup , then Next S

n the empty directory Click on setup , then Next Select Install from Internet , then click on Next Use the default install root directory, which should be c:/cygwin Select 'Default Text File Type' of DOS, then Next Use the default Local Package Dire

DOS, then Next Use the default Local Package Directory, which should be the directory you created above. If you are not behind a firewall, click on direct connection, then click Next Select a nearby mirror, then click Next You will be presented wit

mirror, then click Next You will be presented with a list of packages to install. The initial default installs packages from Base , which is roughly a 6Mb download that expands into about 26Mb of files. If you are serious about development you may

iles. If you are serious about development you may also want to install the packages below. To install an individual package, click on the + to the left of the group name, then click on the arrows to the right of the group name

the arrows to the right of the group name until Default changes to Install Devel Category autoconf - requires gawk and m4 from the Interpreters Category binutils, gcc, gettext, libintl libintl1, libtool, libtool-devel - The matlab interf

tl1, libtool, libtool-devel - The matlab interface requires gcc and Matlab. You may find it easier to install all of the Devel category cvs Net Category openssh - cvs may need this openssl - required by openssh Text Category: less Hit Next , w

quired by openssh Text Category: less Hit Next , which should download the packages and install them Click on Add icon to Start Menu , and then Finish . Start up bash with Start -> Cygnus Solutions -> Cygwin Bash Shell c:/cygwin/etc/passwd is created

Cygwin Bash Shell c:/cygwin/etc/passwd is created during the Cygwin installation. If your Windows account is a domain account and not a local account, then you may need to add an entry to c:/cygwin/etc/passwd by hand. Use the mkpasswd command to a

/etc/passwd by hand. Use the mkpasswd command to append a line with your login information, for example, I used: mkpasswd -d -u cxh --path-to-home=/cygdrive/c/users >> /etc/passwd mkpasswd -h will print out help for the mkpasswd command Set the value

nt out help for the mkpasswd command Set the value of the PTII environment variable to the name of the directory where Ptolemy II is installed. Under Windows 2000, you set the environment variable via the Environment tab of the System control panel (

the Environment tab of the System control panel ( Start Menu -> Settings -> Control Panels -> System -> Advanced -> Environment Variables The PTII value should be set using back slashes so that javac gets the right path if you've installed Cygwin in

gets the right path if you've installed Cygwin in a location other than C:\ such as C:\Cygwin . If you have installed Cygwin in c:/cygwin, and your Ptolemy II installation is in a directory outside of the c:/cygwin tree, then Cygwin may change your

f the c:/cygwin tree, then Cygwin may change your PTII variable to include "/cygdrive/c". This will cause problems with non-Cygwin tools like javac that do not understand /cygdrive/c An example PTII value would be: c:\users\yourname\ptII Cygwin dire

value would be: c:\users\yourname\ptII Cygwin directory mapping Note that Cygwin remaps directories, so d:\tmp may appear as /cygdrive/d/tmp . Unfortunately, the java command does not understand the Cygwin /cygdrive notation, so java may not find the

ygwin /cygdrive notation, so java may not find the files it needs. For example, pwd returns /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII , which will not work with java : cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ PTII=d:\\tmp\\ptII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ echo $PTI

II cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ echo $PTII d:\tmp\ptII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ cd $PTII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ pwd /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII So, under Cygwin, it is best to avoid using pwd when setting variables. Avo

st to avoid using pwd when setting variables. Avoid: PTII=`pwd`/ptII and use PTII=d:\\tmp\\ptII instead. Note that the configure script checks for this sort of thing. In the Environment tab, you should also set your path to include the location of

uld also set your path to include the location of the JDK bin directory (i.e. C:\jdk1.3\bin ) When you run cygwin.bat, your path should be set to include the Cygwin tools. You can test this by typing type make , which should return make is /usr/bin/

type make , which should return make is /usr/bin/make If make cannot be found, then you may also need to add the Cygwin bin directory c:\cygwin\usr\bin to your path. Optional: Install JavaCC, see the instructions above . Start up Cygwin bash , and c

instructions above . Start up Cygwin bash , and cd to the Ptolemy II directory. Run rm -f config.* Run ./configure Run make fast install Now type vergil (or $PTII/bin/vergil if you did not set your path) in the C shell or cygwin Bash window and the

ath) in the C shell or cygwin Bash window and the Ptolemy II GUI should start up Currently, Ptolemy II does not support building via Microsoft Visual Studio nmake under Windows. Also, we have not tried building Ptolemy II under Windows '95 or Window

ed building Ptolemy II under Windows '95 or Windows '98. The instructions for setting up a parallel tree using CVS can be found in $PTII/doc/coding/develsetup . Last Updated: $Date: 2002/06/14 02:24:39 $
